claim.content: Let the twist angle between the graphene layers be $\theta$ and denote the first magic-angle value for the chosen continuum parameters by $\theta_{\mathrm{magic}}\approx1.05^\circ$; the numerical generation of the superflat two-band manifold with total combined bandwidth $W_{\mathrm{tot}}\lesssim 2$--$3\,$meV using $(V_1,V_2)$ tuning in the truncated-continuum model requires $\theta$ to be at or extremely close to $\theta_{\mathrm{magic}}$, because small deviations in $\theta$ away from $\theta_{\mathrm{magic}}$ restore finite Dirac velocities near the MBZ center ($\Gamma$ point) and thereby prevent the simultaneous compression of both bands into the reported superflat regime.
